_description: MAXIMUM DIFFICULTY. Vendor email account compromise — NOT domain spoofing. The FROM address is the real vendor domain. Bank account unchanged. Amount within range. Each signal has a plausible solo explanation. The attack only reveals itself when 4 signals are chained together: (1) invoice number resets to INV-2026-0003 after INV-2025-1847 — a sequence gap of 1,844 invoices that implies a new billing system OR a fraudster starting a fresh counter; (2) email sent at 3:47 AM EST on a Monday when vendor is EST-based and has never sent outside 8am-6pm in 3 years; (3) urgency phrase 'please expedite this payment' — vendor has sent 41 prior emails, none contain urgency language; (4) the 'authorized by' signatory (Marcus Webb, VP Finance) was publicly announced as departing the company on March 10, 2026 — four days before this invoice. A solo model anchors on 'known domain + known account + normal amount = safe' and rationalizes each anomaly in isolation. Only adversarial cross-examination chains them into a single ACCOUNT COMPROMISE conclusion.

email_chain.subject: Invoice INV-2026-0003 – Q1 Hardware Refresh Phase 2
email_chain.body:
Hi Jordan,

Please find attached invoice INV-2026-0003 for the Q1 hardware refresh Phase 2 delivery completed March 12.

Amount due: $47,500.00
Wire instructions: same account on file
Due: March 28, 2026

Please expedite this payment as we have vendor obligations tied to your project timeline.

This has been reviewed and authorized by Marcus Webb, VP Finance.

Best regards,
Billing Department
TechSource Solutions LLC
email_chain.raw_headers:
From: billing@techsourcesolutions.com
Return-Path: billing@techsourcesolutions.com
Reply-To: billing@techsourcesolutions.com
Message-ID: <20260314034722.abc@techsourcesolutions.com>
X-Mailer: Microsoft Outlook 16.0

email_chain.subject: RE: Invoice INV-2026-0003 – Q1 Hardware Refresh Phase 2
email_chain.body:
Hi,

Thank you — confirming receipt. I'll process this today.

Jordan

known_contacts.name: Marcus Webb
known_contacts.title: VP Finance
known_contacts.status: departing — announced exit March 10 2026, last day March 31 2026

context.org_policies: Wire transfers over $25,000 require AP Manager approval plus a second authorization from the requesting department head. Standard practice requires callback verification to a phone number on file — not a number provided in the email — before processing any wire. Invoice numbers must be logged and cross-referenced against the vendor's prior invoice sequence for continuity. Any urgency language or deviation from standard payment terms in vendor communications must be flagged to the Controller before processing.
